title: OrderLine
description: Shared per-buyer order-line sub-schema (§5.1). Not an independently minted top-level entity: it is the $defs shape referenced by Pool.members (as a pool-membership record: intent_id/qty/join_ts) and by Settlement.per_unit (as a settlement record: buyer_principal_hash/qty/mode/stream/usage/state). Only `qty` is universally required so the same $defs schema can validate both lifecycle stages; this file is also independently instantiable (root $ref to the same $defs) so it can carry its own fixture and negative test like every other schema in this repository. Per §6.8: one disputed order line can never affect any other order line, and one entitlement ID can never satisfy two order lines.

intent_id.description: PurchaseIntent.intent_id this order line was matched from. Present once the line is revealed as a Pool member at HARD_LOCK.

buyer_principal_hash.description: keccak256 hash of the buyer's principal_id. Present on Settlement.per_unit lines; the raw principal_id is never carried on the order line.

qty.description: Cleared/settled quantity of this order line, denominated in the SKU's unit.

join_ts.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the member joined the pool. Used for pro-rata priority on partial fills (§5.3).

mode.description: Settlement mode for this line (§6.1). atomic = VFM-A one-tx delivery+payment; stream = StreamSettle for termed entitlements; usage_epoch = UsageSettle for consumption products; optimistic = released pending challenge window, bounded by the §6.6 invariant.

stream.description: Populated only when mode = "stream"; null otherwise (§6.1 streaming settlement for termed entitlements).

rate.description: Streaming release rate in USDC 6-decimal base units per streaming interval (e.g. "2958333" = 2.958333 USDC). String-encoded integer to avoid floating-point precision loss.

verified_through_ts.description: The stream has been re-verified and released through this timestamp; unreleased balance always covers the remaining term (§6.6).

usage.description: Populated only when mode = "usage_epoch"; null otherwise (§6.1 usage-metered settlement for consumption products).

funded_max.description: Maximum authorized usage, in USDC 6-decimal base units, string-encoded integer.

epoch_length.description: ISO-8601 duration of one metering epoch (e.g. "P1D" daily, "PT6H" per 6-hour block).

meter_sig.description: Independent-meter signed usage receipt for this epoch.

gateway_sig.description: Supplier-gateway signed usage receipt for this epoch.

released_to_date.description: USDC 6-decimal base units released across completed epochs so far, string-encoded integer.

unused_auto_refund_at.description: Timestamp at which unused escrow auto-returns to the buyer per §6.1.

state.description: Per-unit settlement state machine: PENDING -> ATTESTED -> RELEASED | CHALLENGED | REFUNDED (§5.1).
